calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-21-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Continued To Jun-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Bresler for (petitioner) Yingya Tan.

The Court stated that this is a spousal property petition, that there are problems in that it is held as separate property and that the funds did not come out of a community estate. Mr. Bresler stated that he has an unsigned declaration that the petitioner's father provided the money to her. The Court stated that it was put into the husband's name. Mr. Bresler stated that this was required by the title company because Ms. Tan was unemployed and that he has a declaration which could be formalized and signed. The Court stated that it is not sure it will sway the Court as the Court has to deal with the legal formalities. Mr. Bresler suggested that they might get the title company to clear things up. The court stated that it is not sure what the title company could say, that it is not their business to tell people how to hold property, that the matter can be continued to present additional evidence but that based on what is before it it will be denied. The petition for spousal property was continued to June 21, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for supplemental evidence.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
